title: malus sylvestris var. bulgarica
intro: Malus sylvestris var. bulgarica, also known as the Bulgarian wild apple, is a subspecies of the wild apple tree native to Bulgaria and the surrounding regions. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its astringent and anti-inflammatory compounds, which are used to treat a range of health issues. The health benefits of Malus sylvestris var. bulgarica include reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and promoting wound healing, making it a popular choice in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves, flowers, and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health complaints.
